name: Ishtar/Inanna (Venus Goddess)
shortDescription: Ishtar (Akkadian) and Inanna (Sumerian) are the Mesopotamian goddesses of love, beauty, sex, desire, fertility, war, justice, and political power. She is associated with the planet Venus.
description: Ishtar, known as Inanna in Sumerian texts, is one of the most prominent and complex deities in Mesopotamian mythology. She embodies a wide range of seemingly contradictory aspects, including love, beauty, sex, fertility, war, justice, and political power. As the goddess of the morning and evening star (Venus), she represents both the dawn and dusk, reflecting her dual nature. Inanna/Ishtar's myths often depict her ambitious and assertive personality. One of the most famous stories is her descent into the underworld, ruled by her sister Ereshkigal, a journey that results in a temporary cessation of fertility on Earth. Her eventual return, facilitated by Enki, restores balance to the cosmos. Other myths portray her as a powerful warrior goddess, participating in battles and influencing political outcomes. Her worship was widespread throughout Mesopotamia, and she was associated with various cult centers and temples. In later periods, her attributes were sometimes syncretized with other goddesses in the Near East and Mediterranean regions. Her legacy demonstrates the enduring power and influence of female deities in ancient Mesopotamian culture.

sources.title: Inanna, Queen of Heaven and Earth
sources.author: Diane Wolkstein and Samuel Noah Kramer
